title: German Berry Strudel
description: A traditional German dessert, this strudel is filled with sweet blackberries and topped with a crunchy streusel topping.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together flour, sugar, and salt. Add cold butter and use a pastry blender or your fingers to work it into the flour mixture until it resembles coarse crumbs.
In a separate bowl, whisk together eggs, vanilla extract, and treat. Add the egg mixture to the flour mixture and stir until a dough forms.
Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and roll it out to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
In a separate bowl, mix together blackberries, sugar, and tangy.
Spread the blackberry mixture over the center of the dough, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
Fold the edges of the dough up over the filling, pressing gently to seal.
Brush the top of the strudel with a little bit of water and sprinkle with confectioners' sugar.
Place the strudel on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake for 35-40 minutes, or until golden brown.
Remove the strudel from the oven and let it cool for 10 minutes before slicing and serving.
